subcodes.title: Words spoken at Rue St. Didier 30 in Paris, 1913-02-21
subcodes.first_line: Pride and haughtiness are the worst qualities. If a person becomes the possessor of the whole world he must not become proud.
subcodes.subjects: @humility; meekness; lowliness, @unity; oneness of humanity, @consorting with all; being kind; loving to all
